Refrigerate for 1 hour and serve chilled.
notes: The avocado creates a rich, creamy texture while adding healthy fats.

name: Chocolate Chia Mousse
preparation: Blend almond milk, cocoa powder, maple syrup, and vanilla until smooth.
Pour over chia seeds and stir well.
Refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight.
Blend the set chia mixture until mousse-like and smooth.
Serve in glasses topped with dark chocolate shavings.
notes: Blending the set chia pudding transforms it from a pudding into a smooth mousse.

name: Chocolate Pudding Light
preparation: Mix cornstarch and cocoa powder with 1/4 cup cold milk.
Heat remaining milk with sugar until warm.
Add the cocoa-cornstarch slurry, stirring constantly.
Cook on medium-low heat until thick and creamy.
Add vanilla extract, pour into bowls, and chill for 2 hours.
notes: Cornstarch gives a silky smooth texture without needing eggs or heavy cream.

name: Banana Nice Cream
preparation: Slice bananas into coins and freeze for at least 4 hours.
Add frozen banana chunks to a food processor.
Add almond milk, honey, and vanilla.
Blend until smooth and creamy like soft-serve.
Serve immediately or freeze for 1 hour for firmer texture.
notes: The key is using very ripe bananas with brown spots for maximum sweetness.

name: Mango Nice Cream
preparation: Add frozen mango chunks and banana to a food processor.
Add coconut milk and honey.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Serve immediately as soft-serve or freeze for 1 hour for firmer texture.
Garnish with fresh mango cubes if desired.
notes: Frozen fruit is the key; fresh fruit will not achieve the ice cream consistency.

name: Berry Nice Cream
preparation: Add frozen bananas and berries to a food processor.
Add almond milk and honey.
Blend until creamy and smooth, scraping down sides as needed.
Serve immediately or freeze for 1 hour for firmer scoop.
Top with fresh berries if desired.
notes: Berries give this nice cream a beautiful vibrant purple color naturally.

name: Peanut Butter Nice Cream
preparation: Add frozen banana chunks to a food processor.
Add peanut butter, almond milk, and honey.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Fold in dark chocolate chips.
Serve immediately or freeze for 1 hour for firmer texture.
notes: Use natural peanut butter with no added sugar for the healthiest version.

name: Protein Brownies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a small baking pan.
Mix oat flour, protein powder, and cocoa powder.
Add mashed banana, honey, and almond milk; stir until combined.
Pour into the lined pan and spread evenly.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until set but still fudgy. Cool before cutting.
notes: Do not overbake; they should be slightly fudgy in the center for the best texture.

name: Black Bean Brownies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and grease a small baking pan.
Blend black beans until smooth.
Add cocoa powder, honey, coconut oil, baking powder, and vanilla; blend again.
Pour into the pan and spread evenly.
Bake for 20-22 minutes until set. Cool completely before cutting.
notes: You cannot taste the beans at all; they create an incredibly fudgy texture.

name: Avocado Brownies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a small baking pan.
Mash avocado until completely smooth.
Mix in cocoa powder, honey, oat flour, baking soda, and vanilla.
Pour into the lined pan and spread evenly.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until set. Cool completely before slicing.
notes: Avocado replaces butter entirely, adding healthy fats and a super fudgy texture.

name: Fruit Sorbet Mango
preparation: Add frozen mango chunks to a food processor.
Add honey, lime juice, and water.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Transfer to a container and freeze for 2 hours, stirring once after 1 hour.
Scoop and serve with fresh mint leaves.
notes: Lime juice enhances the mango flavor and prevents the sorbet from being too sweet.

name: Fruit Sorbet Berry
preparation: Add frozen berries to a food processor.
Add honey, lemon juice, and water.
Blend until smooth.
Strain through a fine sieve to remove seeds if desired.
Freeze for 2 hours, stirring once, then scoop and serve.
notes: Straining removes raspberry seeds for a smoother texture, but it's optional.

name: Fruit Sorbet Lemon
preparation: Make simple syrup by heating water and sugar until dissolved. Cool completely.
Mix cooled syrup with fresh lemon juice and zest.
Pour into a shallow dish and freeze.
Scrape with a fork every 30 minutes for 2-3 hours until fluffy.
Serve in chilled glasses.
notes: The most refreshing palate cleanser; perfect between courses or after a heavy meal.

name: Fruit Sorbet Watermelon
preparation: Freeze watermelon cubes for at least 4 hours.
Blend frozen watermelon with honey, lime juice, and salt.
Process until smooth and creamy.
Serve immediately as soft sorbet or freeze for 1 hour for a firmer scoop.
Garnish with fresh mint.
notes: A pinch of salt intensifies the watermelon's natural sweetness.

name: Oat Cookies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a baking sheet.
Mash banana and mix with oats, honey, chocolate chips, and cinnamon.
Drop spoonfuls of mixture onto the baking sheet and flatten slightly.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until golden.
Cool on the sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
notes: These 2-ingredient base cookies (banana + oats) need no flour, butter, or eggs.

name: Banana Oat Cookies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a baking sheet.
Mash bananas and mix with oats, peanut butter, honey, and cinnamon.
Drop spoonfuls onto the baking sheet and flatten slightly.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until golden around the edges.
Cool on the sheet before removing; they firm up as they cool.
notes: Peanut butter adds protein and helps bind the cookies together.

name: Almond Flour Cookies
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a baking sheet.
Mix almond flour, baking soda, honey, egg white, and vanilla.
Form a dough and roll into small balls, then flatten slightly.
Place on the baking sheet with space between each cookie.
Bake for 10-12 minutes until edges are golden. Cool completely.
notes: These are naturally gluten-free; almond flour gives a delicate, nutty flavor.

name: Peanut Butter Cookies Healthy
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C and line a baking sheet.
Mix peanut butter, honey, egg, vanilla, and baking soda until smooth.
Roll into balls and place on the baking sheet.
Press each ball with a fork to create the classic crosshatch pattern.
Bake for 10-12 minutes until set. Cool on the sheet.
notes: Use natural peanut butter without added sugar or hydrogenated oils.

name: Energy Balls Date
preparation: Blend dates in a food processor until a sticky paste forms.
Add oats, almond butter, chia seeds, and cinnamon; pulse until combined.
Roll into small balls using slightly damp hands.
Place on a tray and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to a week.
notes: Perfect grab-and-go snack; dates provide natural sweetness and instant energy.

name: Energy Balls Cocoa
preparation: Process dates in a food processor until sticky.
Add oats, cocoa powder, almond butter, and honey; blend until combined.
Roll into small balls.
Roll in extra cocoa powder for coating if desired.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving.
notes: These taste like brownie bites but are made from whole, natural ingredients.

name: Energy Balls Coconut
preparation: Process dates until a sticky paste forms.
Add half the coconut, oats, coconut oil, and cardamom; blend until combined.
Roll into small balls.
Roll each ball in remaining desiccated coconut to coat.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes to set.
notes: Coconut oil helps bind the balls and adds a rich tropical flavor.

name: Energy Balls Almond
preparation: Pulse almonds in a food processor until coarsely ground.
Add dates, almond butter, honey, and vanilla; process until combined.
Roll into small balls.
Roll in crushed almonds for extra crunch if desired.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
notes: Almonds are rich in vitamin E and healthy fats, making these a nutritious treat.

name: Greek Yogurt Bark
preparation: Mix Greek yogurt with honey until smooth.
Spread evenly on a parchment-lined baking sheet to 1/4 inch thickness.
Scatter berries, granola, and chocolate chips on top.
Freeze for 3-4 hours until completely solid.
Break into irregular pieces and serve immediately from the freezer.
notes: Store in a freezer bag and eat within a few minutes of taking out as it melts quickly.

name: Frozen Yogurt Bark
preparation: Mix yogurt with honey.
Spread on a parchment-lined tray to about 1/4 inch thick.
Top with sliced strawberries, pistachios, and coconut flakes.
Freeze for 3-4 hours until solid.
Break into pieces and serve straight from the freezer.
notes: Get creative with toppings; this is a blank canvas for any fruit and nut combination.

name: Chocolate Bark Healthy
preparation: Melt dark chocolate in a double boiler until smooth.
Pour onto a parchment-lined tray and spread thin.
Scatter almonds, cranberries, pumpkin seeds, and sea salt on top.
Refrigerate for 1 hour until completely set.
Break into irregular pieces.
notes: Dark chocolate with 70%+ cocoa is rich in antioxidants and lower in sugar.

name: Baked Banana
preparation: Preheat oven to 190°C.
Slice bananas lengthwise without peeling and place on a foil-lined tray.
Drizzle with honey and sprinkle cinnamon.
Bake for 15 minutes until soft and caramelized.
Top with chocolate chips and walnuts while warm; the chocolate will melt.
notes: The natural sugars in banana caramelize beautifully when baked, creating a toffee-like sweetness.

name: Grilled Pineapple
preparation: Slice pineapple into 1/2 inch thick rings and pat dry.
Brush with honey and sprinkle with cinnamon.
Grill on a hot grill pan for 2-3 minutes per side until char marks appear.
The heat caramelizes the natural sugars for extra sweetness.
Serve warm, garnished with coconut flakes.
notes: Grilling intensifies pineapple's sweetness; pairs wonderfully with a scoop of vanilla nice cream.

name: Roasted Peaches
preparation: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Place peach halves cut-side up on a baking sheet.
Drizzle with honey and sprinkle cinnamon.
Roast for 15-18 minutes until soft and caramelized.
Top with chopped pecans and a dollop of Greek yogurt.
notes: Roasting concentrates the peach flavor and creates a natural caramel sauce.

name: Rice Pudding Light
preparation: Cook rice in milk on low heat, stirring frequently.
Continue cooking until rice is very soft and milk is thick and creamy.
Add sugar, vanilla extract, and cinnamon.
Stir well and cook for 2 more minutes.
Serve warm or chilled with a dusting of cinnamon.
notes: Short-grain rice gives the creamiest pudding due to its higher starch content.

name: Coconut Rice Pudding
preparation: Cook rice in coconut milk and regular milk on low heat.
Stir frequently until rice is soft and the mixture is thick.
Add sugar and cook for 2 more minutes.
Pour into serving bowls.
Top with toasted coconut flakes and serve warm or chilled.
notes: Toasting coconut flakes in a dry pan brings out their nutty, aromatic flavor.

name: Cinnamon Rice Pudding
preparation: Cook rice in milk with a cinnamon stick on low heat.
Stir frequently until rice is soft and creamy.
Remove the cinnamon stick, add sugar and cinnamon powder.
Cook for 2 more minutes until well combined.
Serve warm or chilled, dusted with extra cinnamon.
notes: Using both cinnamon stick (for depth) and powder (for warmth) creates a richer flavor.

name: Panna Cotta Light
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Heat milk with sugar until just warm and sugar dissolves.
Add the bloomed gelatin and stir until completely dissolved.
Add vanilla extract and pour into moulds.
Refrigerate for 4-5 hours until set. Unmould and serve with berry sauce.
notes: The perfect panna cotta should wobble when you shake it; do not use too much gelatin.

name: Coconut Panna Cotta
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Heat coconut milk and regular milk with sugar until warm.
Add bloomed gelatin and stir until fully dissolved.
Pour into moulds and refrigerate for 4-5 hours.
Unmould and serve with mango puree or tropical fruits.
notes: Coconut gives a silky texture and tropical flavor to this Italian classic.

name: Mango Panna Cotta
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Heat milk with sugar until warm and sugar dissolves.
Add bloomed gelatin and stir until dissolved.
Let it cool slightly, then mix in mango puree.
Pour into moulds and refrigerate for 4-5 hours until set.
notes: Layer plain and mango panna cotta for a beautiful two-tone presentation.

name: Apple Crumble Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Toss apple slices with half the sugar and cinnamon, place in a baking dish.
Mix oats, flour, remaining sugar, and cold butter until crumbly.
Scatter the crumble topping over the apples.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden and bubbling. Serve warm.
notes: Oats in the crumble topping add fiber and a nice nutty texture.

name: Berry Crumble Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Place berries in a baking dish and toss with 1 tbsp sugar.
Mix oats, flour, remaining sugar, and cold butter until crumbly.
Scatter crumble over the berries.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbly. Serve warm.
notes: Use a mix of blueberries, raspberries, and blackberries for the best flavor combination.

name: Peach Cobbler Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Place sliced peaches in a baking dish and toss with 1 tbsp sugar.
Mix flour, remaining sugar, milk, melted butter, and baking powder into a batter.
Pour the batter over the peaches.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden and set. Serve warm.
notes: Fresh peaches work best, but canned (in juice, not syrup) can be used off-season.

name: Frozen Fruit Popsicles
preparation: Blend fruits with orange juice until smooth.
Mix in yogurt and honey.
Pour into popsicle moulds.
Insert sticks and freeze for 5-6 hours until solid.
Run warm water over the mould to unmould easily.
notes: Layer different fruit purees for a beautiful rainbow effect.

name: Coconut Popsicles
preparation: Mix coconut milk, coconut water, and honey until smooth.
Stir in desiccated coconut.
Pour into popsicle moulds.
Freeze for 5-6 hours until solid.
Run warm water over the mould to release.
notes: A tropical, creamy popsicle that tastes like frozen coconut cream.

name: Yogurt Popsicles
preparation: Mix yogurt, fruit puree, honey, and milk until smooth.
Pour into popsicle moulds.
Insert sticks and freeze for 5-6 hours.
Run warm water over the mould to unmould.
Serve immediately.
notes: Greek yogurt makes these popsicles creamier and higher in protein.

name: Lemon Posset Light
preparation: Heat cream and sugar in a saucepan, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Bring to a gentle simmer for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat and add lemon juice and zest.
Stir well; the acid will cause the cream to thicken.
Pour into small glasses and refrigerate for 3-4 hours until set.
notes: This classic English dessert relies on lemon acid to set the cream - no gelatin needed.

name: Citrus Curd Light
ingredients: 1/8 cup fresh citrus juice (lemon, orange mix)
preparation: Whisk eggs with sugar in a heatproof bowl.
Add citrus juice and zest.
Place over a pot of simmering water (double boiler).
Stir constantly for 8-10 minutes until the curd thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat, stir in butter, strain, and chill.
notes: Press plastic wrap onto the surface while cooling to prevent a skin from forming.

name: Orange Mousse Light
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Gently heat 1/4 cup orange juice and dissolve the bloomed gelatin.
Whisk Greek yogurt with remaining juice, honey, and orange zest.
Stir in the gelatin mixture until incorporated.
Pour into glasses and refrigerate for 3 hours until set.
notes: Fresh orange juice makes a noticeable difference in flavor and color.

name: Meringue Cookies
preparation: Preheat oven to 110°C and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Beat egg whites and cream of tartar until soft peaks form.
Gradually add sugar, one tablespoon at a time, beating until stiff glossy peaks.
Fold in vanilla extract.
Pipe or spoon small mounds onto the sheet and bake for 1.5-2 hours until dry.
notes: Ensure bowl and beaters are completely grease-free; even a trace of fat prevents whipping.

name: Pavlova Mini Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 120°C and line a baking sheet.
Beat egg whites until soft peaks, then gradually add sugar until stiff and glossy.
Fold in vinegar and cornstarch gently.
Spoon 4 individual nests onto parchment with a well in each center.
Bake for 1 hour, turn off oven, and cool inside with door ajar.
Top with Greek yogurt and fresh berries before serving.
notes: Vinegar and cornstarch are the secret - they create a crisp shell with marshmallow center.

name: Meringue Nests Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 110°C and line a baking sheet.
Beat egg whites with cream of tartar until soft peaks, then add sugar until stiff and glossy.
Pipe or shape into nest shapes, building up the sides.
Bake for 1.5 hours until dry and crisp. Cool in the oven.
Fill each nest with lemon curd and top with fresh raspberries.
notes: Fill nests just before serving to keep them crisp; moisture softens them quickly.

name: Stuffed Dates International
preparation: Slit each date lengthwise to create a pocket without cutting through.
Mix mascarpone with orange blossom water and honey until smooth.
Fill each date with about 1 tsp of the mascarpone mixture.
Press chopped pistachios on top.
Chill for 15 minutes before serving.
notes: Orange blossom water is traditional in Middle Eastern desserts; a little goes a long way.

name: Chocolate Dates
preparation: Slit each date open and fill with 1 tsp almond butter.
Press dates closed gently and place on a parchment-lined tray.
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in a double boiler.
Dip each stuffed date halfway into the chocolate.
Sprinkle with sea salt flakes and refrigerate for 20 minutes until set.
notes: The sweet-salty-chocolate combination hits every flavor note perfectly.

name: Tahini Dates
preparation: Slit each date lengthwise to create a pocket.
Mix tahini with cinnamon and sea salt.
Spoon about 1 tsp of tahini mixture into each date.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the filled dates.
Serve at room temperature or chill for a firmer texture.
notes: Tahini and dates are a classic Middle Eastern pairing; the nuttiness balances the sweetness.

name: Coconut Macaroons Healthy
preparation: Preheat oven to 165°C and line a baking sheet.
Whisk egg whites with salt until foamy but not stiff.
Fold in shredded coconut, honey, and vanilla.
Scoop rounded tablespoons onto the baking sheet.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden. Cool completely on the sheet.
notes: Naturally gluten-free; coconut provides all the structure in place of flour.

name: Almond Macaroons
preparation: Preheat oven to 165°C and line a baking sheet.
Whisk egg whites and salt until foamy.
Fold in almond flour, honey, and almond extract.
Roll into small balls, flatten slightly, and place on the sheet.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until golden outside but soft inside. Cool on sheet.
notes: Almond extract intensifies the nutty flavor; skip it for a more subtle taste.

name: Hazelnut Macaroons Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 165°C and line a baking sheet.
Beat egg whites with salt until foamy.
Fold in hazelnut meal, maple syrup, and vanilla.
Drop rounded tablespoons onto the baking sheet.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until golden with slightly cracked tops. Cool on sheet.
notes: Toast hazelnut meal briefly in a dry pan before mixing for a deeper, nuttier flavor.

name: Acai Bowl Dessert
preparation: Break frozen acai packets into chunks and add to a blender with banana and almond milk.
Blend until thick and creamy like soft-serve, using a tamper if needed.
Add honey and blend briefly.
Pour into bowls.
Top with granola and mixed berries. Serve immediately.
notes: The key to a thick acai bowl is minimal liquid and keeping everything frozen until blending.

name: Smoothie Bowl Dessert
preparation: Blend frozen banana, berries, yogurt, and almond milk until thick and smooth.
Keep the consistency thick; add milk only a splash at a time.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with chia seeds and sliced almonds in rows.
Serve immediately while cold and thick.
notes: Freeze banana slices flat on a tray first; they blend more smoothly.

name: Pitaya Bowl Dessert
preparation: Break frozen pitaya into pieces, add to blender with banana and coconut milk.
Blend until thick and creamy, scraping sides as needed.
Add honey and pulse briefly.
Transfer to bowls.
Top with coconut flakes and sliced kiwi. Serve immediately.
notes: Pitaya (dragon fruit) is naturally lower in sugar than acai, making it a lighter base.

name: Chia Jam On Toast
ingredients: 1/2 cup fresh or frozen mixed berries
preparation: Heat berries in a saucepan until they soften and release juices, about 5 minutes.
Mash with a fork, then stir in chia seeds and honey.
Let sit for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until jam-like consistency.
Toast the bread slices until golden.
Spread almond butter on toast, then top with chia jam.
notes: Chia seeds naturally gel, creating jam texture without pectin or excess sugar.

name: Fresh Fruit Tart Light
ingredients: 1/4 cup mixed fresh fruits (kiwi, berries, mango)
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C.
Mix almond flour, coconut oil, and 1 tbsp honey into a dough and press into a tart pan.
Bake the crust for 12-15 minutes until golden, then cool completely.
Whisk yogurt with 1 tbsp honey and spread over cooled crust.
Arrange sliced fresh fruits decoratively on top. Chill 30 minutes before serving.
notes: Use the freshest seasonal fruits for the best flavor and vibrant presentation.

name: Berry Tart Light
preparation: Preheat oven to 175°C.
Mix oat flour, coconut oil, and maple syrup into a dough and press into a tart pan.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until firm and golden, then cool.
Mix ricotta with honey until smooth and spread over cooled crust.
Arrange mixed berries on top. Chill 20 minutes before serving.
notes: Oat flour crust is sturdier; blend rolled oats in a food processor to make your own.

name: Matcha Pudding
preparation: Mix cornstarch with 1/4 cup almond milk to make a slurry.
Heat remaining milk until warm.
Sift matcha powder and whisk into the warm milk until dissolved.
Add cornstarch slurry and honey, stirring constantly until thick.
Add vanilla, pour into dishes, cover surface with plastic wrap, and chill 2 hours.
notes: Sift matcha before adding to prevent clumps; ceremonial grade gives the smoothest flavor.

name: Matcha Nice Cream
preparation: Add frozen banana chunks to a food processor.
Add matcha powder, almond milk, and honey.
Blend until smooth and creamy like soft-serve.
Scoop into bowls and top with chopped pistachios.
Serve immediately or freeze for 1 hour for firmer texture.
notes: Slice bananas before freezing; they blend much more easily.

name: Green Tea Mousse Light
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Warm the cream slightly and dissolve the bloomed gelatin.
Sift matcha into the cream and whisk until smooth.
Mix yogurt with honey, then fold in the matcha cream mixture.
Divide into glasses and refrigerate for 3 hours. Dust with matcha before serving.
notes: Folding (not stirring) the cream into yogurt keeps the mousse light and airy.

name: Tiramisu Light
preparation: Whisk light mascarpone, yogurt, and honey until smooth and creamy.
Briefly dip each ladyfinger into cooled espresso; just a quick dip, not a soak.
Arrange a layer of dipped ladyfingers in a serving dish.
Spread half the mascarpone mixture over the ladyfingers.
Repeat layers, dust generously with cocoa, and refrigerate for 4+ hours.
notes: Chilling overnight lets the flavors meld; tiramisu always tastes better the next day.

name: Coffee Mousse Light
preparation: Bloom gelatin in warm water for 5 minutes.
Dissolve espresso powder in 1 tbsp warm water.
Warm the cream and stir in bloomed gelatin until dissolved.
Mix yogurt, honey, and espresso, then fold in the cream-gelatin mixture.
Pour into glasses and refrigerate for 3 hours. Dust with cocoa powder.
notes: Instant espresso dissolves more smoothly than ground coffee for a cleaner flavor.

name: Espresso Granita
preparation: Brew espresso and stir in honey and vanilla while still hot until dissolved.
Add a pinch of cinnamon.
Pour into a shallow freezer-safe dish and cool to room temperature.
Place in freezer and scrape with a fork every 30 minutes for 2-3 hours.
Continue until the entire mixture is fluffy ice crystals. Serve in chilled glasses.
notes: Use the widest, shallowest pan you have for faster, more even freezing.

name: Carrot Cake Bites
preparation: Squeeze excess moisture from grated carrots with a paper towel.
Combine carrots, oats, almond butter, honey, cinnamon, and ginger.
Mix thoroughly until the mixture holds together.
Roll into tablespoon-sized balls.
Refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up. Store in fridge for up to 5 days.
notes: Squeeze moisture from carrots for firmer bites that hold their shape better.

name: Air Fryer Apple Chips
preparation: Slice apple very thin (use mandoline if possible).
Sprinkle with cinnamon.
Arrange in single layer in air fryer.
Air fry at 160°C for 12-15 min, flipping once.
Cool completely for crunch.
notes: Naturally sweet, crunchy chips.

name: Air Fryer Banana Fritters
preparation: Dip banana slices in besan batter with cardamom.
Spray and air fry at 180°C for 8-10 min.
Serve warm.
notes: Warm banana treat.

name: Air Fryer Coconut Balls
preparation: Mix coconut, condensed milk and cardamom.
Shape into small balls.
Air fry at 160°C for 8 min until golden.
Cool before serving.
notes: Golden, fragrant coconut ladoo.

name: Air Fryer Pear Chips
preparation: Slice pear thin.
Sprinkle with cinnamon and nutmeg.
Air fry at 160°C for 14 min, flipping once.
Cool for crunch.
notes: Delicate, naturally sweet chips.

name: Air Fryer Sweet Shakarpara
preparation: Mix flour, sugar, ghee and cardamom.
Knead into stiff dough.
Roll and cut into diamond shapes.
Air fry at 170°C for 12-14 min until golden.
Cool completely.
notes: Crunchy festival treat, less ghee.

name: Air Fryer Malpua Bites
preparation: Mix flour, milk, sugar, cardamom and fennel into batter.
Drop small spoonfuls in air fryer.
Air fry at 170°C for 8-10 min.
Serve warm.
notes: Mini malpua without deep frying.

name: Air Fryer Stuffed Dates
preparation: Stuff each date with almond butter and top with almond half.
Air fry at 160°C for 5-6 min until warm.
Sprinkle sea salt.
notes: Warm, gooey, naturally sweet.

name: Air Fryer Ragi Cookies
preparation: Mix all ingredients into a dough.
Shape into small cookies.
Air fry at 160°C for 10-12 min.
Cool on wire rack.
notes: Healthy millet cookies.

name: Grilled Pineapple with Honey
preparation: Grill pineapple rings for 3-4 min per side until char marks appear.
Drizzle with honey.
Sprinkle cinnamon and pepper.
Serve warm.
notes: Caramelised, naturally sweet dessert.

name: Grilled Banana with Jaggery
preparation: Place banana halves on grill cut-side down.
Grill for 3-4 min until caramelised.
Top with grated jaggery, cardamom and coconut.
notes: Simple Indian-style grilled dessert.

name: Grilled Peach with Curd
preparation: Grill peach halves cut-side down for 4 min.
Top with yoghurt and honey.
Garnish with mint.
notes: Light, refreshing grilled fruit dessert.

name: Grilled Mango with Chilli-Lime
preparation: Grill mango slices for 2-3 min per side.
Squeeze lime juice.
Sprinkle chilli and salt.
Serve warm.
notes: Sweet, spicy, tangy grilled mango.
